# Questions in Livestream 192

Live Stream #192: Truths, Facts, and Blessed Facts

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=431" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:11</a> | Is <em>War of the Gods</em> going to be a series? | Yes. He said the second book was already written and the third was partly done. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=481" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:01</a> | Are these books delaying progress on the Ten Plagues book? | No. He said work on the Ten Plagues book was still ongoing, though they still needed a publisher to accept it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:58</a> | Is this your science-fiction series? | Yes. He described it as science fiction, roughly a cross between Sherlock Holmes and Indiana Jones in outer space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=623" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:23</a> | Will the book be available in paperback or hardcover? | Both, along with an ebook. He said the hardcover would have a dust jacket. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:24</a> | Are not all relics relics of the dead? | No. He said some relics are associated with divinities or deities, though many medieval European relics were of the dead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=973" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:13</a> | Would Indiana Jones-style fedoras be good for desert travel? | No. He said they were made more for the American West, are hot in desert conditions, and better desert options exist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1103" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:23</a> | Why did God make people? | He answered that God creates in order to have relationship with his creation, including humanity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1168" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:28</a> | What are your thoughts on the show <em>Expedition Unknown</em>? | He called it a poor show that investigates ancient aliens, paranormal topics, and archaeology in a way he compared unfavorably with <em>Ancient Aliens</em>. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1218" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:18</a> | What is Mr. E's in-universe origin? | He joked that Mr. E spawned from the endless possibilities of "could be." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1345" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:25</a> | How much would it take for you to do a live playthrough of the new Indiana Jones game? | He said video game playthroughs take him away from writing, so they are billed at his normal consulting rate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1422" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:42</a> | How much would it take for you to review a book? | He said book reviews are different: they usually do not charge a consulting fee, but the requester needs to send the book, and he was backlogged. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:47</a> | Would watching edited game cutscenes on YouTube work instead of a playthrough? | He said that is not really what they do, and video-game reviews also run into issues such as copyright music and the cost of the game. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:27</a> | Did you get your view of the Nephilim by reading a lot of Bronze Age literature? | Yes. He said repeated exposure to ancient Near Eastern literature led him to see the Nephilim as a priestly caste. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:10</a> | Where did you get your view that true prophets of God do water miracles? | He said ancient Near Eastern literature presents water manipulation as a sign of a prophet with power, and he pointed to biblical examples involving Moses, Joshua, Elijah, and Jesus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=1946" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:26</a> | Is water miracle imagery connected to water being chaotic in ancient Egyptian cosmology? | Not necessarily. He said water was viewed as difficult to control, and manipulating large amounts of water carried unusual weight. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=2089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:49</a> | Why are the historical dates you quote often later than the dates found in common reference works? | He said chronology has shifted substantially in recent years and that his dates follow more recent recalibration and computer-generated synchronism work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=2531" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:11</a> | Is there a book that integrates biblical history from Terah and Abraham through the conquest with mainstream science, or could you write one? | He recommended Richard Hess's book on ancient Israelite religion as the closest option and said he would like to write such a book if he had time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=2649" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:09</a> | As vizier, could Joseph have gone back home to see Jacob? | He said a vizier would have had unusual freedom to travel inside Egypt, though he was unsure exactly what "home" meant in the question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=2909" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:29</a> | In Leviticus 10:16-20, what is Aaron's defense and why does it work? | He treated it as a question about the aftermath of Aaron's sons' deaths and explained Aaron's response in that ritual context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=3339" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:39</a> | Are Levites Israelites, and how does Numbers 26:64 fit with Eleazar being counted? | He said Levites are Israelites, but they are normally excluded from censuses because they are not counted for ordinary military or tax obligations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=3546" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:06</a> | Could Levites volunteer for service even though it was prohibited to conscript them? | Yes. He said it was prohibited to conscript Levites, even though some did serve. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=3562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:22</a> | Was the census mainly for counting fighting men, and is that why Levites were excluded? | He said censuses were used for military conscription and corvee labor, and Levites were exempt from those ordinary obligations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=3655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:55</a> | Why does Genesis 10:13-14 list Philistines as descended from Egypt, and is there historical truth to this? | He said Genesis 10 is ethnography rather than a genetic or straightforward historical account, categorizing peoples by cultural association. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=3907" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:07</a> | What Christmas traditions does the Fulk family have? | He said they keep a fake Christmas tree up year round, partly because no one gets around to taking it down. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=4378" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:58</a> | How outdated is Kenneth Kitchen's book <em>The Bible in Its World</em>? | He said it is dated, but it aged fairly well and contains early forms of Kitchen's core ideas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=4621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:01</a> | When did the Hebrew or Jewish belief in reincarnation develop? | He said it appears to be a later Jewish development, probably Common Era or perhaps late Second Temple, but the timing is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=4710" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:30</a> | Why are the bells on the high priest's robe there so he will not die in Exodus 28:34-35? | He said the bells and pomegranates function as hem weights to preserve modesty while the priest ministers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=4891" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:31</a> | In Exodus 31:17, how is God "refreshed"? | He said the language is not about God being physically rejuvenated, but about ceasing from labor, transcending creation, and admiring the completed work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=5486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:26</a> | Are there publishers that let an author skip a book proposal and just give them the book? | He said a few do, such as Brill, but most publishers require proposals for planning and editorial reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=5807" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:47</a> | In Exodus 32:32, is Moses asking to die with Israel or in Israel's place? | He said the text is not entirely clear, but Moses is pleading for forgiveness and offering himself as part of that intercession. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=5978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:38</a> | Why do early Exodus daters say Rameses has been switched out for Avaris when the Bible says the Israelites built Rameses? | He said they use redaction or later-renaming explanations to preserve the early date, but that creates compounding problems with multiple fixed historical references. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=6395" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:35</a> | What do you mean when you say the Song of the Sea is after the Exodus? | He said parts of the song appear to reflect later stages after the wilderness period and into the conquest, while likely preserving an earlier Mosaic core. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=6787" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:07</a> | What does Exodus 34:14 mean by saying God's name is jealous? | He said "jealous" is better understood as possessive in covenant context: God wants what is his and expects Israel to keep the covenant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7083" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:03</a> | Would accepting a historical Exodus and conquest diminish the view that El and Yahweh were originally separate deities? | He said that is probably reasonable, since the Exodus traditions consistently present Yahweh as the actor on Israel's behalf. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:47</a> | Where did the Israelites get calendar names such as Abib? | He said Israel seems to have adopted month names from the Canaanite calendar, with the Gezer calendar as an example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:48</a> | In Exodus 34:24, does the second "he" refer to Moses or God? | He said he could not identify the grammatical issue in the English or Hebrew and had to pass on the question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7413" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:33</a> | What is the significance of Jesus walking on water? | He connected it to water miracle imagery as a sign of a true prophet's power to manipulate water. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7452" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:12</a> | Were ear rings or nose rings common in ancient Egypt? | Earrings were very common for men, women, and kings; he said nose rings were exceptionally rare and he could not think of an Egyptian example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7573" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:13</a> | Were Zoroastrians astrologers? | He said apparently not, based on what he has read from Zoroastrian scholars, though Hittites did practice astrology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7659" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:39</a> | If Hittites are from Anatolia, which is not east of Israel, how does that relate to coming from the east? | He explained that ancient direction language often reflects ethnic geography rather than compass coordinates, so northward travel around the Fertile Crescent could be conceptualized as eastward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7942" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:22</a> | If the Magi came from the east, where were they probably from? | He said they were probably from Turkey, while cautioning that the evidence does not let us know with certainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=7975" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:55</a> | Did archaeologists exist in ancient Egypt? | He pointed to Khaemwaset as the closest ancient Egyptian example because he investigated, restored, and preserved older monuments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=8102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:02</a> | How does Exodus 6:17 tell us the long lives refer to clans? | He said it does not state it directly, but the demographics require abbreviated clan genealogies rather than only three literal generations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=8679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:39</a> | Who is Molech, and why is a ram set aside for it in Leviticus 18:21? | He said Molech is best understood as a kind of sacrifice rather than a god, and he did not see a ram reference in Leviticus 18:21. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=9015" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:15</a> | Is the story of Cain and Abel an ancient tale of works versus faith? | He rejected that reading as eisegesis and said the story is better read in context as involving pastoralism, agrarianism, and Israel's religious program. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=9259" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:19</a> | Do we equivocate the definition of wisdom in the Old Testament, since Exodus 35:26 seems to denote skill or competency? | He agreed that Hebrew <em>hakma</em> can mean skill, competency, and practical wisdom, such as the ability of a craftsman. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=9397" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:37</a> | Have you read Kim Ryholt's work on the Second Intermediate Period, and how do you assess his seriation chronology? | He said Ryholt gathered useful data, but the book is now badly dated and its conclusions, especially about the Fourteenth Dynasty, are not dependable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=10120" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:40</a> | Did you see Wes Huff's appearance on Joe Rogan, and what are your thoughts? | He had not watched it yet, so he could not comment beyond saying it was on his watch list. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=10237" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:37</a> | Who is the most underrated pharaoh? | He chose Amenhotep I for restoring stability, reforming government, and setting conditions for later New Kingdom success. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=10383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:03</a> | If Egyptian gods were believed to act through their idols, does that correlate with Genesis saying God created man in his image? | Yes. He said the Genesis language uses terms associated with religious images, fitting the idea of humanity as God's image in his temple creation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=10529" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:29</a> | How much was a talent worth in Matthew's parable? | He said a talent is a large weight of metal rather than a fixed coin value, and in Matthew 25 it represents a very large amount of wealth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=10886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:26</a> | In Hosea 11:5, is it possible to read <em>lo</em> as "surely" instead of "not"? | He said it is possible in some contexts, but in Hosea 11:5 the syntax and historical context favor the normal negative reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=11255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:35</a> | What are your thoughts on Hebrews 11:26-27 mentioning Christ and "the one who is invisible"? | He said Hebrews presents Christ as the fulfillment and payoff of the Old Testament promises, greater than even Moses' vision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=11547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:27</a> | How do you interpret Psalm 137:8-9? | He treated the passage as hyperbolic siege language, describing judgment on Babylon in the same terrible terms Babylon had brought on others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=12099" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:39</a> | Does the Hebrew word <em>adam</em> mean red in a way that proves Adam's skin color? | No. He said the argument is too simple, that <em>adam</em> means man or humanity, and that Brown-Driver-Briggs is dated for this kind of claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=12313" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:13</a> | How do we know the date of Khufu, and is it historical or radiometric? | He said Khufu is dated historically through king lists, reign lengths, and related sources, not by radiometric dating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=12657" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:30:57</a> | Are camels an anachronism in the patriarchal narratives? | No. He said evidence for camel domestication goes back far earlier than Abraham's likely period, so the objection is badly outdated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=12811" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:31</a> | Based on winged goddesses on Egyptian ritual furniture, were the cherubim on the ark female? | Probably. He said the late Bronze Age configuration was likely female winged figures facing each other and forward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=12899" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:59</a> | How do throne or sacred-space guardians such as lamassu, cherubim, seraphim, wadjet figures, and sphinxes compare? | He said these are not all equivalent: cherubim and seraphim perform different sacred-space functions, wadjet is a winged solar-serpent motif, and sphinxes symbolize royal power rather than a normal worshiped being. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=13131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:51</a> | Is it significant that the lifespans from Adam through Moses add up to 12,600 years? | He answered no, saying the Genesis genealogies are complicated by honorific transposition and textual variants, so they should not be treated as a simple arithmetic chain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=13408" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:28</a> | How were genealogies tracked in the Old Testament and the ancient Near East? | He said Israelite genealogies often track clan identity with abbreviated relationships, while Egyptian and Mesopotamian practices vary by context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=13647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:27</a> | Did ancient Egyptians have poems or stories that did not involve their gods? | He said basically no; Egyptian stories nearly always involve divine elements in some way, much as Greek epics do. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=13837" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:37</a> | Do we really know only a tiny fraction of ancient Egypt? | Broadly yes. He said we have the rough skeleton of Egyptian history, but most daily administrative and court-life detail is still missing and most of Egypt remains unexcavated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=13998" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:18</a> | Do we know much about Roman history? | He said we know more about Roman political history and administration than about Egyptian government because more Roman political writing survived. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_5SSVZ0_soc?t=14051" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:11</a> | Which ages in Abraham's story do you interpret as clans, which as persons, and was Isaac his son or another descendant? | He said the evidence is not clear enough to decide all details, but the very long ages point to clan-level interpretation rather than only one long-lived individual. |
